question: What inservice inspection capabilities does the NuScale RPV design provide?

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III, 2013 Edition standards, and clarifies the classification of various piping and components according to their safety significance.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for compatibility and stress corrosion cracking mitigation. It also outlines that preservice and inservice inspections and testing of pressure-retaining components are conducted according to the latest ASME BPVC Section XI requirements, as mandated by 10 CFR 50.55a, with inspection intervals and code editions specified in the inspection program.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet the regulatory requirements of 10 CFR 50, including General Design Criteria for quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture prevention. The materials used for Class 1 components, such as the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and steam generators (SGs), are specified in Table 5.2-4, ensuring compliance with all relevant material and testing standards.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details how the steam generators are integrated within the reactor vessel, forming part of the primary and secondary pressure boundaries, and references where further design specifics can be found in other report sections.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with NRC regulations and ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) requirements for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details the use of specific ASME code cases for design, fabrication, inspection, and testing, ensuring all selected code cases meet NRC-endorsed conditions and are properly documented in referenced tables.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing validates the new support design, and the most likely degradation mechanism remains wear, with secondary corrosion risks assessed as low and manageable within standard plugging criteria.

edges.description: Section A establishes that the RPV and its components, including threaded fasteners, comply with ASME BPVC requirements for inspection, while Section B provides specific details about the materials and design of those fasteners in accordance with the same code, thus Section B specifies how Section A's general compliance is achieved for fasteners.

Section A asserts compliance with ASME BPVC Section III for inspection and construction of the RPV, while Section B lists the ASME NQA-1 standard as a reference, which provides quality assurance requirements supporting the implementation of those inspections.

Section A describes the materials and standards used for RPV threaded fasteners and references Section B, which lists the specific industry standards and guidelines (such as ASME and EPRI documents) that inform those material and inspection requirements.

Section A describes the materials and their compliance with ASME and regulatory requirements for the RCPB, while Section B specifies that the RPV and associated components are designed, constructed, and inspected according to those same ASME and regulatory standards, thus Section A provides material-specific details that support the broader compliance claims made in Section B.

Section A establishes that RCPB materials, including those in the RPV, conform to ASME BPVC requirements, while Section B specifies that threaded fasteners (a component of the RPV) also meet these material standards and are compatible with the inservice environment, thus providing detailed evidence supporting the RPV's inservice inspection capabilities.

Section A describes the RCPB materials' compliance with ASME and regulatory requirements and mentions that details are provided elsewhere, while Section B lists references, including ASME and EPRI documents, that support or are cited for these requirements.

Section A describes the components and subsystems included in the NuScale RPV design, while Section B specifies that these components are designed and inspected in compliance with ASME Code and regulatory requirements, thus linking the design features to their inspection capabilities.

Section A describes the components and subsystems included in the NuScale RPV design, while Section B specifies the materials and inspection/testing requirements for threaded fasteners used in those RPV components, connecting the design features to their inservice inspection capabilities.

Section A describes the components and subsystems included in the NuScale RPV design, while Section B lists references that provide standards and guidelines relevant to the integrity and quality assurance of those components, connecting them through the regulatory and technical basis for design and inspection.

Section A specifies the material standards and regulatory compliance for the RPV, which are foundational to the design features and components described in Section B, thereby ensuring that the RPV's inservice inspection capabilities are based on compliant materials and construction.

Section A references Section B by discussing compliance with the same ASME BPVC codes and regulatory requirements (10 CFR 50.55a) that Section B details for the RPV and associated components, establishing the regulatory framework for inservice inspection capabilities.

Section A references the inservice test program and compliance with regulatory requirements, while Section B provides specific details about the materials and inspection requirements for RPV threaded fasteners, connecting general compliance in A to specific inspection capabilities in B.

Section A discusses compliance with ASME and NRC requirements for inservice testing and inspection, while Section B lists references (such as ASME NQA-1 and EPRI guidelines) that support or are cited by Section A's discussion of these regulatory and technical requirements.

Section B establishes that the NuScale RPV and associated components are designed and inspected in compliance with ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a, while Section A discusses specific design features and operational experience relevant to inservice inspection, thus Section A provides supporting detail for the general compliance claim in Section B.

Section A discusses the design and degradation mechanisms of reactor coolant system components, while Section B details the materials and inspection requirements for RPV boundary components, both providing information relevant to the inservice inspection capabilities of the NuScale RPV design.

Section A discusses the observed degradation mechanisms and inspection considerations for reactor coolant system components, while Section B lists references to industry standards and guidelines that inform or support the inspection and integrity requirements mentioned in Section A.

Section A references the inservice test program and compliance with regulatory requirements, while Section B discusses observed degradation mechanisms and program guidelines for reactor coolant system components, both providing information relevant to the inservice inspection capabilities of the NuScale RPV design.
